MATH 5110 Interactive Book Overview

Applied Linear Algebra and Matrix Analysis — from linear systems to vector spaces, spectral theory, inner-product geometry, matrix factorizations, data science, networks, optimization, probability, and matrix calculus.

Open QMD

Course knowledge map

Click any topic. Blue arrows show a natural learning path: computation $\to$ abstraction $\to$ spectral structure $\to$ geometry $\to$ applications.

How to use this page

Interactive guide

Click a box in the map, or search the chapter list. The panel will show the topic's role in the book and useful links.

Linear algebraMatrix analysis

Book narrative

This book begins with solving $Ax=b$. It then asks: What are the spaces behind the equations? What are the transformations behind the matrices? What structures survive a change of coordinates? Eigenvalues reveal invariant directions; inner products add geometry; projections produce least squares; factorizations make computation reliable; SVD and FFT transform data; graph Laplacians organize networks; optimization, probability, and matrix calculus connect linear algebra to modern AI and data science.

1. Compute
Linear systems, row reduction, matrix algebra, LU, determinants.
2. Abstract
Vector spaces, subspaces, bases, coordinates, linear maps.
3. Decompose
Eigenvalues, diagonalization, Jordan, Schur, spectral theorem.
4. Project
Inner products, projections, QR, least squares, Hilbert spaces.
5. Apply
SVD, PCA, FFT, graphs, TDA, optimization, probability, matrix calculus.
27+chapters and application topics
3lab modes: QMD, HTML, notebook
4viewpoints: algebra, geometry, computation, data
AIcompanion activities throughout

Chapter and lab links

Independent study workflow

Read
Start with the story and definitions.
Compute
Run the Python examples and vary inputs.
Interact
Use the HTML lab to test intuition visually.
Prove
Open hide/show proofs only after trying.
Reflect
Ask AI for a similar example, then solve it yourself.
